key_themes: Strong revenue growth, profitability, and market share gains in UK business energy sector
Robust net cash position (£80m+), rising dividends (~3.9% yield), and healthy balance sheet
Attractive valuation (P/E ~8.6x) with analyst targets implying 30%+ upside (avg ~£23.14)
Positive 2026 guidance including 21-25% revenue increase and record Q1 performance
Continued order book expansion and investment for future growth
summary: Yü Group PLC (YU.L), a UK business energy and metering supplier, reported strong 2025 results with significant revenue growth, adjusted EBITDA of £48.8m, higher EPS, and a substantial net cash position.[[1]](https://www.yugroupplc.com/investors/)[[2]](https://shareprices.com/news/yu-group-ups-dividend-as-profit-climbs-starts-2026-with-momentum-7s0eocvhdozyime/) The company has delivered consistent market share gains, an expanding order book, and upbeat guidance for 2026 with revenue expected at £850-875m.[[3]](https://global.morningstar.com/en-gb/news/alliance-news/1768911935006526800/trading-updates-capital-meets-top-end-of-view-yu-group-revenue-rises) Analysts maintain Strong Buy ratings with price targets well above the current ~£17.30 level, citing undervaluation and growth momentum, though retail social media discussion on X remains sparse.[[4]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/YU.L/)
sources: 10+ web news/RNS articles (Yahoo Finance, LSE, Investing.com, company site), ~5-10 X posts scanned (very limited relevant investor discussion; mostly unrelated or announcements)
